title: CricketStudio OKF Claim Object
description: Schema for a single atomic claim in the CricketStudio claim ledger, served at https://players.cricketstudio.ai/claims.jsonl. Every claim traces to a ball-by-ball source with an explicit metric definition, sample size, and date window. Required for OKF Level 3 (Agent-Safe) conformance.

id.description: Stable permanent identifier for this claim. Format: cs_claim_{slug}_{metricId}. Never reused; append-only ledger.

metric.description: The OKF metric this claim uses. Must correspond to a metric defined in /metrics.json (players.cricketstudio.ai).

value.description: The numeric or string result of the claim. Null when suppressed by sample-size floor.

period.description: Human-readable date window for this claim (e.g. 'IPL 2026', 'last 5 matches', '2026-04-12 to 2026-06-01').

comparator.description: Optional rank or comparison context (e.g. '#1 among qualified bowlers', 'above league average').

sampleSize.description: Number of deliveries or events backing this claim. Zero means the claim was suppressed by the sample-size floor.

computedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this claim was computed from ball-by-ball data.

headline.description: Atomic claim prose under 30 words. Follows the OKF atomic claim format: [Entity] [metric] [value] [comparator] [period].

context.description: Optional supporting sentence providing match or situational context for the claim.

pillar.description: Content pillar assignment: P1=match recaps, P2=moments, P3=form & phase, P4=season comparatives, P5=notebook.

provenance.description: Source attribution. Value: 'CricketStudio ball-by-ball aggregation' for all live data. See /trust-manifest.json for full provenance chain.

canonicalUrl.description: Stable canonical URL of the CricketStudio page where this claim appears. Permanent per URL doctrine (CLAUDE.md §4).

examples.headline: Jasprit Bumrah conceded 7.12 runs per over in IPL 2026 (342 balls, #3 among qualified bowlers).
examples.context: Qualification floor: 15 balls bowled in phase.
